tax: Placed at family rank, Cuprous Metalkin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a conductive native-metal body fed by ore-seam. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a conductive metal body with its own discharge mode.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a bright metallic ribbon or coil. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a verdigris smear and arc-scorch on damp rock — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Cuprous Metalkin occupies copper-seamed wet galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where earth-charge animates an ore-seam and recur with the seam re-grows the ribbon, sustaining themselves on earth-charge through conductive ore.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: poses as exposed ore, discharges through wet stone. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: ductile conductive body and field-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: discharge and constriction. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Cuprous Metalkin matters because kills wet-booted crews on the gallery floor. It lets survey reports sort conductive hazards from magnetic ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Cuprous Metalkin is the family-rank grouping of conductive native-metal elementals of the copper kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a ductile conductive metal body that discharges earth-charge, and divide below into genus groups by metal purity and coil form. The only earth elementals that hunt with current.
